comments.reader: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in reading. She is developing her reading skills with growing confidence and can access texts at the appropriate level for Year 4. Asel's reading fluency is improving and she is becoming more accurate in her reading aloud, though continuing to build expression and pace will support her comprehension. The key area for Asel to focus on is inference — drawing conclusions about characters' feelings and motives from evidence in the text. This skill will unlock deeper understanding and enjoyment of the books she reads. Building her reading vocabulary through wide reading will also strengthen both her comprehension and her writing. Reading every day from engaging texts at the right level of challenge will be central to Asel's continued progress in Year 5.
comments.writer: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in writing. She approaches her writing with care and is developing her skills across the range of genres covered in Year 4. Asel shows growing confidence in her spelling of key year group patterns and her handwriting is becoming more fluent and joined. The main areas for Asel to develop are her composition skills — building stronger connections between her ideas and expressing them with greater clarity and purpose. Her punctuation and grammar accuracy, particularly with fronted adverbials and inverted commas for direct speech, needs continued attention to support her meaning-making. Drawing more actively on her reading for vocabulary choices and structural ideas will be an important next step. With focused practice on these key skills, Asel will be able to show more of her potential as a writer in Year 5.
comments.mathematician: Asel has achieved the age-related standard in mathematics. She is a developing mathematician who has built solid foundations across the key areas of Year 4. Asel's times table knowledge is excellent — she has strong fluency with her multiplication facts up to 12×12, which is a real strength that supports her work on fractions and division. She is developing her written calculation methods and shows growing accuracy with column addition, subtraction and short multiplication. The key areas for Asel to focus on are her mathematical reasoning and problem-solving skills — building her confidence to explain her thinking and tackle unfamiliar challenges. Her strong times table fluency gives her a firm foundation to build on as the mathematical demands increase in Year 5, particularly in more complex fraction work and multi-step problems.
